Why are you interested in this role?: I genuinely like being the reliable person in the middle—the one candidates, hiring managers, and the People team all count on to keep things moving. At InGenius Prep I coordinate logistics and scheduling for 20+ programs across Marketing, Tech, and Sales teams, and managing a high volume of moving pieces with seamless communication is exactly where I do my best work (my supervisor has called me the most organized person he's ever worked with). A Recruitment Coordinator role lets me bring that to a fast-scaling team while doing what I love most: making people feel taken care of through a smooth, human process.
Tell us about your experience supporting: At Kronos Research I coordinated a high volume of recruiting touchpoints—drafting job descriptions, posting openings, organizing recruiting events, and delivering a personalized candidate experience that helped increase applications by 566% in two months. At Vestas I coordinated interview schedules and communications with senior leadership across employee relations and headcount projects, and at InGenius I keep our ATS and documentation accurate while juggling cross-functional calendars daily. Managing many calendars and a large volume of interviews at a demanding pace is genuinely something I'm good at and enjoy.
What ATS/tools have you used?: I have extensive hands-on Greenhouse administration experience—posting roles, scheduling interviews, emailing candidates, and managing the pipeline and data. I also use Asana, Notion, and Google Workspace daily to stay organized across workstreams. I'll be candid that I haven't used Cohere's exact stack yet, but I pick up new platforms quickly and enjoy it, and I'm genuinely interested in applying AI to make recruiting coordination smarter—something I already do in my own work.
Why Cohere?: I'm drawn to Cohere because the mission—scaling intelligence to serve humanity—pairs ambition with a real sense of responsibility, and because you're at the stage where a Recruitment Coordinator has visible impact on who joins and how the team grows. My graduate training in Applied Organizational Psychology taught me to treat hiring as a system to study and improve, and I'd be excited to bring that mindset to a team moving as fast as yours.
